channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Meta Plans Major Layoffs to Fund $600 Billion AI Infrastructure
item.description: Meta Platforms is reportedly planning substantial layoffs, potentially impacting over 20% of staff, to help fund its ambitious $600 billion investment in AI infrastructure by 2028. CEO Mark Zuckerberg is pushing for AI-driven efficiency and 'personal superintelligence,' but the plans come amid internal AI development issues and market questions about AI's ROI. The cuts could affect around 16,000 employees, marking a significant restructuring.

item.title: Meta Plans Up to 20% Job Cuts to Fund AI Infrastructure
item.description: Meta Platforms is reportedly planning significant job cuts, possibly affecting 20% or more of its employees. The move aims to offset massive investments in artificial intelligence infrastructure and leverage AI-assisted efficiency. These layoffs would be Meta's largest since its 2022-2023 'year of efficiency' cuts, which eliminated over 20,000 roles. The company's large AI push includes a projected $600 billion data center investment by 2028 and faces AI model development challenges and fierce competition.

item.title: Bitcoin Faces Targeted Attacks, But TOR Unexpectedly Strengthens Defenses
item.description: Bitcoin's network is built to withstand widespread cable failures, needing over 70% of undersea cables to go down before major disruption. However, a targeted attack on key connection points could be effective with just 20% cable failure, and even less if major hosting providers are affected. Surprisingly, increased use of TOR for privacy has inadvertently strengthened the network by concentrating nodes in secure European countries with robust infrastructure.

item.title: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund: Big Banking Bet Boosts Returns, Risks Mount
item.description: The HDFC Flexi Cap Fund's February 2026 portfolio shows a heavy 34.16% concentration in banking stocks, much higher than most flexi-cap peers. Despite this sector focus, the fund has historically achieved strong returns, beating the Nifty 500 TRI benchmark over various periods thanks to a long-term view and careful stock picks. It holds a moderate Sharpe ratio (1.46) and low portfolio turnover (9.45%), suggesting a buy-and-hold approach. However, this heavy reliance on financials, combined with recent fund manager changes and market volatility, poses potential risks.

item.title: India's Energy Crisis: Coal Returns as Gas Supply Dips, Billions in Assets at Risk
item.description: Tensions in West Asia have severely disrupted India's liquefied natural gas (LNG) supplies, forcing the Union Power Ministry to rely more on coal for electricity. An estimated 7.6 GW shortfall in gas power capacity is expected. The crisis worsens the financial strain on India's gas sector, with billions in assets at risk of becoming obsolete, while the country faces higher energy import costs and fiscal challenges, complicating its energy transition goals.

item.title: US Probes India's Supply Chains Over Forced Labor
item.description: The U.S. Trade Representative has launched investigations into India and 59 other countries over allegations they fail to ban imports of goods made with forced labor. The probe covers major sectors like steel, automotive, and electronics, marking a significant policy shift by the U.S. to reshape global supply chains and potentially disadvantage nations with alleged labor exploitation. India is assessing the situation's impact.

item.title: JSL Industries Secures Tax Victory: Demands, Penalties Cleared
item.description: JSL Industries Ltd. has won a significant tax dispute, with authorities dropping all demands, interest, and penalties after a CESTAT order. The company stated the resolution has no financial impact, closing a matter related to professional expenses.

item.title: India Housing Boom: Stocks Lagging Despite Record Sales
item.description: India's housing sector is booming, with sales hitting new highs and luxury demand soaring. Yet, companies positioned to benefit, such as CERA Sanitaryware, Info Edge, and Home First Finance, are not seeing their stocks keep pace. These firms face challenges from high valuations and specific operational issues, leading to investor caution even as the property market thrives.

item.title: Meta Acquires Moltbook for AI Autonomy Amidst Competition
item.description: Meta Platforms has acquired Moltbook, a social network for AI agents, to bolster its Superintelligence Labs and explore autonomous AI interactions. This move occurs as Meta faces scrutiny over its AI development delays, significant capital expenditures, and intensifying competition from rivals like Google and OpenAI. Moltbook's platform offers a unique environment for AI-to-AI communication, a key frontier in the rapidly evolving AI agent landscape, but also highlights inherent platform security and regulatory risks. The acquisition signals a deeper strategic focus on AI's operative potential beyond content generation.

item.title: Marriott Bonvoy partners with Swiggy to earn travel points on India orders
item.description: Marriott Bonvoy is partnering with Swiggy, India's leading food and grocery delivery service. Members can now earn loyalty points on everyday spending, including food orders and Instamart grocery purchases. Top Marriott Bonvoy members will also get free Swiggy One memberships, turning daily expenses into opportunities for future travel.

item.title: Indian Firms Win ₹12,166 Cr Orders: Infra, Telecom, Renewables Boom
item.description: Indian companies have collectively won new orders exceeding ₹12,166 crore across infrastructure, water management, telecom, renewable energy, and mining. VA Tech Wabag secured a water treatment project, while HFCL landed a global optical fiber contract. KEC International, ACME Solar, Diamond Power Infrastructure, and Jindal Steel also reported significant wins, showing strong industrial activity and economic expansion.

item.title: Fintech Credit Faces Risk as India Bans Real-Money Gaming
item.description: India's burgeoning fintech lending sector faces escalating risks, compounded by the recent nationwide ban on real-money online gaming. While fintechs have rapidly expanded unsecured personal loans, particularly small-ticket amounts, default rates are climbing. This regulatory shift and the inherent vulnerabilities of unsecured credit paint a concerning picture for lenders, especially NBFCs, and highlight potential systemic stress despite overall banking sector stability.

item.title: DCM Shriram Secures $90M IFC Funding to Fuel ESG Chemical Growth
item.description: DCM Shriram has secured $90 million from the International Finance Corporation (IFC) using sustainability-linked debentures. This funding is for expanding its chemicals and agricultural operations, highlighting a strategic commitment to ESG principles. The move positions the company for growth in key sectors and aligns financial goals with sustainable development.

item.title: Fitch Lifts India GDP Forecast to 7.5%, Cites Oil Price Outlook
item.description: Fitch Ratings has boosted India's GDP growth forecast to 7.5% for the current fiscal year and 6.7% for the next. This upgrade stems from strong domestic demand, driven by consumer spending and investment. Fitch also forecasts global oil prices to average $70 a barrel in 2026, while noting that geopolitical risks could still impact prices. The outlook signals continued economic momentum, balanced with managing inflation.

item.title: India Equity Rally Faces AI and Oil Price Shocks
item.description: India's stock market is rebounding, fueled by strong corporate earnings (Nifty 500 profits up 16% in Q3 FY26), double-digit credit growth, and 125 basis points in RBI rate cuts. However, global shifts like rapid AI adoption and rising West Asia tensions are creating new challenges. These could affect IT sector demand and jobs. Soaring crude oil prices also threaten inflation, the rupee, and profit margins for oil companies. The Nifty 50 was near 23,151 on March 13, 2026, as these forces shape the market.

item.title: Gold Hits Record Highs: What's Driving the Surge and Risks Ahead
item.description: Gold prices hit record highs above $5,000 per ounce in early 2026. The rally is driven by rising geopolitical risks, steady central bank purchases, strong ETF inflows, and a weaker U.S. dollar. While more gains are predicted, analysts warn of significant volatility and risks to the current upward trend.

item.title: India's Chip Push: Policy Support Faces Execution Hurdles, High Valuations
item.description: India's government is investing ₹1 lakh crore ($11 billion) to build domestic chip manufacturing. Companies like CG Power, Kaynes Technology, and Moschip stand to benefit from this policy support. However, they face major challenges, including high capital costs, strict customer approvals, and steep valuations, as they try to achieve lasting success in a tough global market.

item.title: India's Fertilizer Sector Caught Between Green Hydrogen Goals and Gas Costs
item.description: India is aggressively pushing for a green hydrogen economy by 2030 to reduce reliance on fossil fuels. The fertilizer sector, a key user of hydrogen for ammonia, is positioned to benefit. However, companies are currently battling volatile natural gas prices and escalating input costs, creating significant operational challenges for key players like Coromandel International, Chambal Fertilisers, Deepak Fertilisers, and GNFC.

item.title: Vodafone Idea Seeks Billions from Investors for 5G Network
item.description: Vodafone Idea is in early talks with investors like JSW Group and ST Telemedia for a potential stake sale. The company urgently needs capital for 5G expansion to compete with rivals Bharti Airtel and Reliance Jio. The Indian government, holding nearly half of Vi, seeks a strategic investor to inject funds and expertise into the debt-laden operator, aiming to boost its market share and upgrade infrastructure.

item.title: India's Agri-Futures Markets Hampered by Conflicting Government Policies
item.description: India's agri-futures markets are weakened by conflicting government policies. A mix of market freedom and government control, along with interventions like the Essential Commodities Act, fluctuating minimum support prices (MSPs), and fragmented local markets, distort price signals crucial for farmers to manage risk. Despite growth potential, these issues and low farmer involvement limit the sector's modernization. Greater policy consistency and stronger Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) are needed to unlock the market's full capabilities.
